title: Detective's Arrival
text: Detective Miller arrived at the Pacific Heights mansion at 10 PM. The pearl necklace, valued at $2 million, was last seen in the master bedroom safe.

title: Mr. Blackwood's Alibi
text: Mr. Blackwood, the owner, reported the theft at 11 PM. He had been at a charity gala from 7 PM to 10:30 PM. His alibi was confirmed by the gala host.

title: Groundskeeper's Statement
text: The groundskeeper, James Riley, was seen near the mansion at 9:45 PM. He claimed he was checking the irrigation system.

title: Victoria Crane's Visit
text: Mrs. Blackwood's sister, Victoria Crane, visited the mansion at 8 PM for dinner. She left at 9:30 PM, visibly upset after an argument with Mr. Blackwood.

title: Pawn Shop Report
text: A pawn shop on Lombard Street reported a woman trying to sell a pearl necklace at 11:30 PM. The description matched the stolen item.

title: License Plate Sighting
text: Victoria Crane's car was spotted on Lombard Street at 11:25 PM on the night of the theft. She claims she was getting gas.

title: Security Footage
text: The mansion's security footage showed a figure in the master bedroom hallway at 9:15 PM. The figure was wearing a long coat, despite the warm evening.

title: Victoria's Financial Trouble
text: Victoria Crane had recently lost her job and was facing foreclosure on her home. Mr. Blackwood had refused to lend her money.

title: Photo Lineup
text: The pawn shop owner identified Victoria Crane from a photo lineup as the woman who attempted to sell the pearl necklace.

title: The Long Coat
text: Detective Miller found a long coat in Victoria Crane's car trunk. The coat had mud stains matching the mansion's garden soil.
